content.text: Last updated April 24, 2026
This privacy policy explains how we process personal data when you visit our website, contact us, sign up for membership or events, or otherwise interact with our organization's services.
We strive to process personal data in an open and transparent manner, giving concise and clear information about how we handle your data.
For the purposes of this policy, “personal data” refers to any information that relates to an identified or identifiable individual.
This notice applies to all parts of our website and related services including public pages, any member or event portals, contact forms, and internal communication features.
Some specific services may have supplementary notices if additional data processing occurs (for example, when using third-party media like embedded videos or newsletters).
The organization behind this website is the data controller for the personal data processed under this notice.

content.what: Identity and contact information for the organization
content.why: To inform users who is responsible for the processing activities described on this page
content.how: Presented through this notice, our contact page, and official communication channels

content.text: The data we process depends on how you use the website. Some information is provided by you directly (for example when you register or make a request), while other information is collected automatically by the site or service.
You may provide information to us when you fill out forms, register for services or events, subscribe to newsletters, or communicate with us through email or messaging features.
When you contact us, we may collect your name, email address, subject line, message content, and any attachments or other information you provide.

content.what: Contact details, message content, attachments and follow-up communication
content.why: To respond to your inquiries, document communication, and continue relevant dialogue where needed
content.how: Submitted by you through forms, email, or other channels and handled by our authorized staff or volunteers

content.text: If our site supports user accounts, we may process login information, display names, contact details, role or membership status, preferences, and other profile data.

content.what: Account credentials, profile information, roles and preferences
content.why: To create and maintain accounts, authenticate users, assign appropriate permissions, and personalize your experience
content.how: Collected during registration, profile updates, and normal use of member features

content.text: We may process information related to your membership status, group affiliations, volunteer roles, event attendance, or participation in our activities.

content.what: Membership status, role affiliation, participation records
content.why: To manage access to member areas, facilitate collaboration, and keep accurate administrative records
content.how: Collected through account setup, event signups, and administrative workflows

content.text: If you apply to volunteer or participate in certain programs, we may process the information you provide in that application (such as motivation, availability, experience, and related details).

content.what: Application content, practical availability, stated interests and relevant background information
content.why: To evaluate applications, communicate about opportunities, and coordinate practical participation
content.how: Submitted through application forms or follow-up communication, reviewed by authorized coordinators

content.text: If you subscribe to newsletters or mailing lists, we may process your email address, language preference, consent status, and records of when and how you signed up.

content.what: Email address, language preference, consent records
content.why: To deliver newsletters and confirm that subscription and communication preferences were handled correctly
content.how: Collected through subscription forms and confirmation flows

content.text: Some information is collected automatically when you visit the website or interact with our services, for example through your browser, device, or our security systems.
We may process technical metadata such as your IP address, browser type and version, language settings, operating system, screen resolution, and timestamps of requests made to the site.

content.what: IP address, browser metadata, language settings and technical request details
content.why: To render the website correctly for your device, improve compatibility, diagnose faults, and maintain site stability and security
content.how: Generated automatically by your browser and our servers when you load pages or request resources

content.text: We may process logs related to login attempts, failed requests, abuse prevention, security events, and administrative actions.

content.what: Authentication attempts, server logs, moderation records and security events
content.why: To detect misuse or attacks, investigate issues, protect the service, and document relevant operational activity
content.how: Collected through our application and infrastructure logging systems and internal security tools

content.text: Depending on your consent choices and the services in use, we may process information stored in cookies or similar tracking technologies on your device.
In line with Norway's updated Ekom law (effective January 2025), any use of cookies and similar technologies requires valid GDPR consent to be lawful.
Essential cookies may store information such as language preferences, interface choices, consent settings, session identifiers, or other settings required for core functionality of the website.

content.what: Locale, session state, and core preference settings
content.why: To keep the website functional and preserve your active choices while using the service
content.how: Stored in the browser by the application when needed for basic site operations

content.text: If optional analytics are enabled, third-party analytics tools may process information about page visits, navigation paths, feature usage, and aggregated behavior signals.

content.what: Traffic patterns, page visits and aggregated usage data
content.why: To understand how our site is used and to improve content, structure and usability over time
content.how: Collected by analytics scripts or services after you have given consent where required

content.text: When our pages include embedded third-party content (such as videos, social media widgets, or maps), those providers may receive technical information (like your IP address) and may set their own cookies depending on your interaction and settings.

content.what: IP address, browser metadata and interaction data related to embedded content
content.why: To display external content (videos, maps, social feeds, etc.) and enable those services to function properly
content.how: Transferred directly between your browser and the third-party provider when the content is loaded or played

content.text: We process personal data only for specific purposes and only when we have a valid legal basis. The applicable basis depends on the feature or interaction (for example: performance of a service, compliance with a legal obligation, our legitimate interests, or your consent).
We process communication data (messages, inquiries, and related personal data) in order to answer questions, provide support, coordinate activities, and document important follow-up.
If you contact us before engaging in any specific arrangement or participation, we may process your information to evaluate and respond to your request or inquiry.

content.what: Initial contact details and request context
content.why: To determine whether and how we can respond to your question or provide the requested information
content.how: Handled through our contact channels, internal notes and relevant follow-up communication

content.text: If communication with us continues over time, we may retain relevant correspondence so that future replies are accurate, consistent, and informed by prior context.

content.what: Message history and related notes
content.why: To provide continuity, avoid repeated explanations, and ensure prior context is not lost
content.how: Stored in our communication tools or administrative systems with access limited to those handling the matter

content.text: Where the website offers user accounts, events, message boards, or direct communication between users, we process personal data to make those services available and secure.
We process authentication data (such as login credentials and session information) to allow you to sign in securely, maintain sessions, and access the appropriate areas of the service.

content.what: Authentication tokens, user identifiers, session state and access control data
content.why: To keep accounts secure and ensure that users only access the features they are permitted to use
content.how: Processed through the secure authentication flow and session management systems

content.text: When users register for events or activities, we may process attendance records, logistical preferences, and communication needed to organize participation.

content.what: Attendance records, logistics and preference information
content.why: To plan events, coordinate participation, and send updates before, during and after activities
content.how: Managed through event tools, registration forms and organizer communication channels

content.text: If the site includes private messaging or community discussions, we process that content to provide the feature, moderate misuse, and respond to any reports.

content.what: Message and post content, moderation records and metadata
content.why: To enable communication features, maintain community safety, and enforce our rules if needed
content.how: Stored and processed within our messaging or discussion system and reviewed when necessary for moderation or support

content.text: In some cases, we may process or retain information to comply with legal obligations, respond to official requests, or document decisions and actions in our organization.

content.what: Required records, audit trails and legally relevant documentation
content.why: To comply with law, defend legal claims if needed, and ensure accountable administration
content.how: Handled through secure internal systems, recordkeeping routines, and controlled access policies

content.text: Some of our processing is based on your consent, particularly for optional services like newsletters, analytics tracking, event media publication, or third-party integrations.
When you sign up for our newsletter, we process your email address and subscription preferences based on your active consent.

content.what: Email address, consent status and mailing preferences
content.why: To send updates only to recipients who have actively chosen to receive them
content.how: Recorded through signup confirmations and preference management

content.text: Any optional analytics tracking (for example, site usage metrics not strictly necessary for the site) is enabled only after you give explicit consent where required.

content.what: Consent records and analytics identifiers
content.why: To ensure that optional measurement tools are only used when you have given permission
content.how: Managed through cookie consent settings and conditional loading of analytics scripts

content.text: If we collect photos or videos at events where individuals are identifiable, we will obtain consent or ensure another lawful basis before publishing that media.

content.what: Photographs or video recordings of individuals
content.why: To document activities, share updates, and illustrate the organization's work in a responsible way
content.how: Captured at events and reviewed before publication according to our internal guidelines and the chosen legal basis

content.text: We do not sell personal data. We may share data with trusted service providers and partners when necessary to operate the website, provide features you request, or protect our systems.
We use third-party hosting, storage, deployment, monitoring, and database services. These providers process data as needed to run and secure the site.
Our website content is served through hosting platforms and content delivery networks (CDNs) to ensure availability and performance.

content.what: Technical request data and delivery logs
content.why: To serve pages reliably, optimize availability, and protect against misuse
content.how: Processed by the hosting environment, CDN edge services, and related infrastructure logs

content.text: Data entered through website features may be stored in external database services and backup systems to ensure availability and recovery.

content.what: Stored account data, communication records and backup copies
content.why: To operate the service safely and maintain continuity in the event of technical failure
content.how: Handled through our production database, secure backups, and data retention routines

content.text: We may use security services to reduce spam, prevent abuse, and enhance protection for login and form entry points.
Our contact forms and entry points may use anti-spam measures (such as CAPTCHAs) to distinguish legitimate submissions from automated abuse.

content.what: Technical interaction signals, IP data and abuse-prevention metadata
content.why: To reduce spam, protect services from automated misuse, and keep communication channels functional
content.how: Processed through the anti-spam or CAPTCHA service when forms are loaded or submitted

content.text: We may use security monitoring tools that process limited operational data (like error logs or unusual patterns) to detect incidents and support incident response.

content.what: Telemetry, error traces and unusual activity patterns
content.why: To identify security incidents quickly and protect systems and data
content.how: Collected through our monitoring infrastructure and logged under internal review

content.text: Some pages may include optional embedded content from external providers. Interacting with such content may result in additional data exchange with those providers.
If our site contains embedded videos, the video provider may receive information about you and may process interaction data when you play the video.

content.what: Technical connection details and media interaction data
content.why: To deliver video playback and measure engagement for the embedded service
content.how: Transferred from your browser to the video provider when the embed is loaded or activated

content.text: Embedded maps, social media widgets and similar features may connect to external domains and possibly set cookies or track your interaction.

content.what: IP address, browser metadata and interaction data for embedded features
content.why: To display the external widget or map and provide its intended functionality
content.how: Loaded from third-party servers when your browser requests the embedded content

content.text: Some of our service providers may process data in countries outside your own. Where this occurs, we take steps to ensure appropriate safeguards are in place as required by law.

content.what: Data handled by external providers that may operate globally
content.why: Because some technical and communication services are delivered through international infrastructure
content.how: Managed through contractual safeguards, documented transfer mechanisms, and provider assessments

content.text: We keep personal data only as long as necessary for the original purpose of collection, business continuity, legal obligations, or legitimate security needs.
Records of our correspondence with you may be kept for varying periods depending on the status of the matter (closed, ongoing, or likely to recur).
Simple matters that have been fully resolved and are unlikely to require future reference may be deleted or anonymized after a reasonable period.

content.what: Closed correspondence with no expected future relevance
content.why: To avoid storing personal data longer than needed after the purpose has been fulfilled
content.how: Handled through internal review and routine deletion or archiving processes

content.text: If an issue remains open, disputed, or still relevant to our operations, we may retain the correspondence for longer until the matter is resolved.

content.what: Ongoing case history and follow-up records
content.why: To preserve context and allow responsible handling of unresolved or recurring matters
content.how: Retained in our communication and case-management systems until the issue is closed

content.text: Account-related information is kept for the lifetime of the account and may be retained for a period after account closure if needed for legal, security, or administrative purposes.
For active accounts, core profile data, authentication details, and user preferences are maintained as long as the account is in use.

content.what: Core account data, login state, permissions and settings
content.why: To keep the account functional and maintain continuity of the features you expect
content.how: Stored in our database and authentication systems during account activity

content.text: When an account is closed or left inactive for a long time, some information may be removed or archived, with only limited records retained for system integrity or legal documentation.

content.what: Residual account references and audit entries
content.why: To document that account actions took place and protect against fraud or disputes
content.how: Retained in reduced form according to our internal retention policies

content.text: Security logs are handled according to their purpose. Routine logs may be kept short term, while logs related to incidents may be retained longer.
Standard technical logs (such as server logs and request traces) may be retained for a short period to support troubleshooting, performance monitoring, and service maintenance.

content.what: Server logs, request traces and system health metrics
content.why: To diagnose faults, stabilize the service and review operational performance
content.how: Stored in monitoring systems with limited retention periods

content.text: If a security incident occurs, relevant logs are retained for longer periods to allow investigation, documentation, and remediation.

content.what: Relevant incident traces, security events and investigation materials
content.why: To understand what happened, respond effectively and prevent future incidents
content.how: Stored in secured systems and accessed only by the incident response team

content.text: Under applicable laws, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, restrict, or object to processing of your personal data, as well as the right to data portability and to withdraw consent where given.
You have the right to ask whether we process personal data about you and, where relevant, to request access to that data.
Where allowed by law, you may ask for a copy of the personal data we hold about you, in a format that is understandable and usable.
You may also request information about the categories of data processed, the purposes of processing, retention periods, and any recipients or processors involved.
If any personal data we hold about you is inaccurate or incomplete, you may ask us to correct or update it.
Where applicable, you may be able to update certain profile information directly through your account settings.
If information is stored in our records or databases, any correction request will be reviewed to ensure accuracy and appropriateness of the change.
In certain situations, you may request deletion of your personal data or ask us to restrict processing while we review your request.
Keep in mind that deletion is not always immediate or total. Some data may need to be retained to comply with legal obligations, ensure security, or document past actions.
If you dispute the accuracy or lawfulness of our processing, you can ask us to restrict further processing of your data while we resolve the issue.
You may object to certain processing and withdraw any consent you have given at any time.
If we process data based on our legitimate interests, we will balance your objection against our reasons for processing. You can still object and we will review it.
If you withdraw consent, this does not affect processing that has already occurred. However, it may affect future use of the features or communications that relied on that consent.
If you believe our processing of your personal data is not lawful, you can contact us directly. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the relevant supervisory authority.
We encourage you to contact us first so that we have an opportunity to address your concerns directly and resolve any issues if possible.
You may also contact the Norwegian Data Protection Authority (Datatilsynet) or another relevant supervisory authority in your jurisdiction if you believe your rights have not been respected.
Certain circumstances may require extra considerations, such as when handling data about children, event photography, public contributions, or voluntary surveys.
If our services are available to younger audiences, we aim to minimize any unnecessary collection of personal data from children and encourage parental involvement where appropriate.

If we become aware that personal data of children has been collected without parental consent (when consent is required), we will halt processing of that data and follow up with appropriate measures (such as obtaining consent or deleting the data).
We may document events and activities with photography or video for internal records or public communication.
Photos and videos may be collected to document activities, commemorate important milestones, and illustrate our organization's work to members and the public.

content.what: Visual recordings of events and activities
content.why: To capture real events and communicate what the organization does in practice
content.how: Captured during events, reviewed internally, and handled according to our publication routines

content.text: If we publish photos or videos on public pages or social media, we review them carefully to ensure that the consent, context, and audience are appropriate.

content.what: Selected images or clips intended for public visibility
content.why: To share updates and activities responsibly while respecting privacy
content.how: Reviewed before publication and limited to the appropriate channels and context

content.text: We may use questionnaires, feedback forms, or surveys to learn from our participants, volunteers, or members on a voluntary basis.
Unless we clearly state otherwise, participation in surveys and feedback activities is voluntary, and responses are used only for the specific purpose described.
We may update this privacy policy from time to time to reflect new services, organizational changes, legal developments, or technical adjustments.
If we make material changes to this notice that significantly affect our data processing practices, we may highlight the updates or provide additional notice through our website or other channels.
Editorial improvements, clarifications, and structural updates may be made without separate notice if they do not substantially change the content of this policy.
If you have questions about this policy or wish to exercise your rights, you can contact us using the contact information provided on the website.

content.what: Personal identification details provided in privacy requests
content.why: To verify your identity, protect your information and that of others, and ensure your request is handled correctly
content.how: Processed through our designated contact channels and internal privacy workflows
